basic_profile.legal_system: Civil law; dedicated statutes for foundations and trusts
basic_profile.currency: Balboa (PAB) pegged 1:1 to and used alongside the US dollar; no central bank of issue

capital_controls.value: No domestic currency to defend (dollarized); de jure absence of capital controls not confirmed by a Tier-1 source this pass

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Removed from the FATF grey list (Oct 2023) and the EU AML list; financial-center reputation rehabilitating but residual de-risking persists

tax_residence_basis.value: Territorial taxation: only Panama-source income is taxed; foreign-source income exempt. Resident if >183 days or center of economic interest in Panama

crypto_specific_guidance.value: No published DGI crypto income/capital-gains guidance found; under territoriality foreign-source crypto gains prima facie fall outside the tax net, but this is NOT confirmed by a DGI ruling

inheritance_tax.value: No inheritance, estate, or gift tax

legal_to_own.value: Legal to own and transact, but unregulated (a legal gray area with no dedicated statute in force)

direction.value: Panama's 2022 crypto law was declared unconstitutional (2023); no general crypto law is in force. A newer 2025 bill is under review and not enacted

self_custody_allowed.value: Not prohibited; no VASP/custody statute in force, so no licensing or self-custody constraints exist. No affirmative primary statement located

crs_participation.value: Panama participates in CRS/AEOI; transparency rating upgraded toward largely compliant; reports of 'limited transmission' rather than fully reciprocal exchange

carf_status.value: Panama's CARF status is in conflict across sources and unresolved: one OECD-derived reading lists Panama as NOT among committed jurisdictions, while a 2025 source lists Panama among jurisdictions committed to begin CARF exchanges in 2027/2028/2029. Do not rely on either reading; confirm against the current OECD CARF commitments list
carf_status.note: Conflict surfaced in the 2026-06-16 verification pass; downgraded High->Low and moved to open questions (unresolved claims are not rendered as statements).

beneficial_ownership_registry.value: Private (non-public) beneficial-owner register for Panama entities under Law 129 of 2020, via resident agents

forced_heirship.value: With a valid will, Panama allows freedom of testation: a testator may dispose of property without a reserved portion (no traditional forced heirship). Dying INTESTATE, however, distributes the estate in equal shares to children/descendants (per stirpes), then ascendants, with the spouse — so the absence of a reserve only helps if a will is in place
forced_heirship.note: Reconciled + confirmed 2026-06-16 across IBA + Lexology + Pardini: testamentary freedom vs intestate equal-share.

foundation_recognition.value: Private-interest foundations (Law 25 of 1995) are commonly used in Panamanian succession planning, but whether they place assets outside forced-heirship claims depends on the settlor's/heirs' domicile, situs, marital property, fraudulent-transfer and conflict-of-laws rules and must be confirmed by counsel
foundation_recognition.note: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: reworded - 'assets outside forced heirship' is a legal conclusion.

foreign_will_recognition.value: Mechanics of recognizing/probating a foreign will not confirmed this pass

account_opening_ease.value: Non-resident account opening is discretionary and harder post-2023; enhanced due diligence and documented source-of-funds are decisive; crypto-derived funds face friction even though crypto is legal

investor_residency_programs.value: Residency-by-investment routes exist. The Qualified Investor Visa grants permanent residency via real estate at US$300,000 until 15 Oct 2026 (reverting to US$500,000 after), or US$500,000 in securities, or a US$750,000 fixed deposit. The Friendly Nations Visa is a separate route for designated nationalities. Thresholds shift; confirm against the live immigration decree at application date
investor_residency_programs.note: QIV threshold + Oct 2026 reversion confirmed across multiple sources in the 2026-06-16 verification pass; upgraded Low->Medium.

family_continuity.value: No inheritance/gift/estate tax plus private-interest foundations make Panama a low-friction succession platform, but intestate equal-share rules apply absent planning and foreign-will mechanics are unconfirmed

direction_of_travel.label: cross-border-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: No wealth/inheritance/gift tax + private-interest foundations create a durable succession/estate platform
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Intestate equal-share rules apply absent planning; foreign-will mechanics unverified

direction_of_travel.label: self-custody-ambiguous
direction_of_travel.rationale: Crypto is legal to own with no licensing burden (friendly for individual self-custody), but the enabling law was struck down and there is no statutory clarity, and crypto banking is hard
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Good for self-custody holders not needing local banking; poor for crypto businesses needing licensing

direction_of_travel.label: reporting-heavy
direction_of_travel.rationale: FATF/EU delisting (Oct 2023) and CRS participation, with a beneficial-ownership registry
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: CRS transmission reportedly limited; CARF commitment status is unresolved across sources (see pa-rep-002)

open_questions.question: Whether foreign-source crypto gain is confirmed exempt under territoriality by any DGI ruling
Affirmative legal confirmation of self-custody / VASP status
Reconcile 'no forced heirship' framing with mandatory intestate equal-share distribution; foreign-will recognition mechanics
Current residency-by-investment thresholds and dual-citizenship rule
CARF commitment status — sources conflict (not committed vs committed for 2027/2028/2029); confirm against the live OECD CARF commitments list

watchlist.item: 2025 crypto bill (VASP registration) under review, not enacted

product_routing_notes: Panama users often surface jurisdictional_concentration / mobility questions (territorial tax, residency programs) and estate planning via foundations. Banking fragility for crypto-derived funds is a recurring flag.

change_log.change: Initial profile from parallel research pass. Heavy reliance on Tier-2 (PwC, IBA) corroborated by Tier-1 (MEF, OECD); crypto tax treatment and self-custody flagged as open questions. Direct DGI/Superintendencia primary pages not yet opened.
